findings.title: audit_logs immutability trigger does not cover TRUNCATE — entire ledger can be wiped silently
findings.description: The immutability trigger uses `FOR EACH ROW` and catches only UPDATE and DELETE events. PostgreSQL's TRUNCATE is a DDL-level operation that bypasses row-level triggers entirely. A privileged user (or a bug in a service that has DDL-level access) can execute `TRUNCATE audit_logs` and wipe the entire append-only ledger — hash chain, actor snapshots, and all — without the trigger firing once. The migration's stated goal is to make audit_logs append-only for Cour-des-Comptes compliance; TRUNCATE is the single operation that completely invalidates that guarantee at the DB level.
findings.root_cause: `FOR EACH ROW BEFORE UPDATE OR DELETE` triggers are not fired by TRUNCATE in PostgreSQL; only a statement-level `BEFORE TRUNCATE` trigger can intercept it.
findings.impact: Any role with TRUNCATE privilege on audit_logs (typically the database owner, superuser, or the application role if not explicitly revoked) can silently destroy the entire audit ledger. This directly voids the Cour-des-Comptes audit trail guarantee that DAT-009/OBS-002 were designed to enforce. The hash chain and actor snapshots are also wiped, making post-hoc integrity verification impossible.
findings.suggested_fix:
Add a statement-level TRUNCATE trigger on audit_logs using the same function:
```sql
CREATE TRIGGER audit_logs_no_truncate
  BEFORE TRUNCATE ON "audit_logs"
  FOR EACH STATEMENT EXECUTE FUNCTION audit_logs_immutable();
```
Also revoke TRUNCATE privilege from the application role (defense-in-depth):
```sql
REVOKE TRUNCATE ON audit_logs FROM <app_role>;
```
The `audit_logs_immutable()` function already raises a generic `check_violation` exception regardless of TG_OP, so it will work correctly for TRUNCATE without modification.
acceptance_criteria: TRUNCATE audit_logs raises 'audit_logs is append-only: TRUNCATE is not permitted' (SQLSTATE 23514)
INSERT into audit_logs still succeeds after adding the TRUNCATE trigger
UPDATE/DELETE on audit_logs still raise the same exception as before
findings.notes: PostgreSQL docs: 'Row-level triggers fired for TRUNCATE are a PostgreSQL extension. They are not part of the SQL standard.' In practice, TRUNCATE simply does not fire FOR EACH ROW triggers at all.

findings.title: audit_logs actorId FK retains ON UPDATE CASCADE after migration removes ON DELETE CASCADE — asymmetry can trigger immutability violation
findings.description: The migration's stated rationale (step 5 comment, lines 95-99) is that the previous ON DELETE SET NULL caused a cascade UPDATE on audit_logs rows which would be rejected by the immutability trigger (step 6) — so it was changed to ON DELETE NO ACTION. However the migration then re-adds ON UPDATE CASCADE on the same FK. If a users.id value were ever changed (e.g., by a migration that reassigns primary keys, an admin fix, or a future user-merge feature), the cascade UPDATE on audit_logs would fire the immutability trigger and abort the users UPDATE with a confusing 'audit_logs is append-only' error. The risk is latent since cuid ids are immutable in practice, but the asymmetry contradicts the stated design principle.
findings.root_cause: The migration author correctly removed ON DELETE CASCADE/SET NULL to avoid cascade writes hitting the immutability trigger, but did not extend the same reasoning to ON UPDATE CASCADE.
findings.impact: Any operation that changes users.id (currently none, but possible in a future migration or admin script) will fail with a confusing SQLSTATE 23514 from the immutability trigger, not a clear FK violation message. This could make users table maintenance operations undebuggably hard. Low probability given cuid usage, but the constraint specification is internally inconsistent.
findings.suggested_fix:
Change ON UPDATE CASCADE to ON UPDATE NO ACTION to be consistent with the ON DELETE NO ACTION:
```sql
ALTER TABLE "audit_logs" DROP CONSTRAINT "audit_logs_actorId_fkey";
ALTER TABLE "audit_logs" ADD CONSTRAINT "audit_logs_actorId_fkey"
  FOREIGN KEY ("actorId") REFERENCES "users"("id")
  ON DELETE NO ACTION ON UPDATE NO ACTION;
```
The actorEmail/actorLabel snapshot columns already preserve actor identity for the audit record, so there is no data preservation need for cascading an id update.
acceptance_criteria: audit_logs actorId FK uses ON UPDATE NO ACTION
Attempting to UPDATE users.id raises a FK violation (23503), not an immutability violation (23514)
findings.verification_command: psql $DATABASE_URL -c "SELECT confupdtype FROM pg_constraint WHERE conname='audit_logs_actorId_fkey';" 2>&1 | grep -E 'a|r'
findings.notes: pg_constraint.confupdtype: 'a' = NO ACTION, 'c' = CASCADE, 'r' = RESTRICT. Current state after migration: 'c' (CASCADE). Expected after fix: 'a' (NO ACTION).

findings.title: dat037 cascade UPDATE of task.projectId produces no audit_logs rows — N tasks silently rewritten under Cour-des-Comptes scope
findings.code_evidence:
-- AUDIT IMPLICATIONS (documented for Cour des Comptes): the parent-side CASCADE
-- silently rewrites N task rows in one DDL fire. Today this is invariant maintenance
-- (the task's "true" project IS the parent's), but a future auditor may want a row in
-- audit_logs for each affected task. NOT added here — the existing OBS-002 trigger
-- pipeline doesn't cover system-derived consistency writes (only user mutations
-- through the app), and adding it now would be scope creep.
findings.description: The epics_cascade_projectid_trg and milestones_cascade_projectid_trg AFTER UPDATE triggers silently rewrite task.projectId on potentially many rows (e.g. moving a large epic to another project rewrites all its tasks). The audit_logs table records the parent (epic/milestone) UPDATE, but none of the N cascaded task rewrites appear in audit_logs. A Cour-des-Comptes auditor examining a task's project attribution history has no DB-level evidence of the cascade; they must infer it from the parent's audit entry, which requires cross-referencing epicId/milestoneId and timing. This is acknowledged in the migration comment as deliberate out-of-scope, but the gap is real and worth a future task.
findings.root_cause: The cascade is implemented as an AFTER UPDATE trigger on epics/milestones that issues a silent UPDATE on tasks, bypassing the application's AuditService which is the only path that writes to audit_logs.
findings.impact: Post-hoc audit trail for task project re-attribution via epic/milestone moves is incomplete. A task could appear to have always been in its current project despite having been moved silently. Under Cour-des-Comptes audit review, this is an explainability gap rather than a security hole.
findings.suggested_fix: File a follow-up task (e.g. DAT-037b) to add audit_logs INSERT rows inside the cascade trigger functions for each affected task row, bracketed by a SYSTEM_CONSISTENCY action code. This is non-trivial because the cascade trigger runs at the DB level without access to the app's AuditService; it would require a plpgsql INSERT into audit_logs directly (similar to how OBS-002 inserts RELEASE_DEPLOYED rows). Alternatively, move the cascade logic from a DB trigger into the application service layer (EpicsService.update) where AuditService is already wired.
acceptance_criteria: When an epic's projectId is changed, audit_logs contains one row per affected task with action='TASK_PROJECT_REASSIGNED' or equivalent
The audit chain remains intact (rowHash chains correctly through the new rows)
findings.notes: The migration author explicitly documented this gap. Severity is suggestion only because the parent's audit row provides indirect derivability and the comment discloses the limitation.

verified_clean: dat005: Float→NUMERIC(5,2/6,2) conversions use USING cast with backup tables pre-created in companion migration 20260524100000; no silent data loss on precision reduction
dat005: No monetary columns in schema — 'money uses Decimal' checklist item N/A per migration comment
dat005: time_entries.hours NUMERIC(5,2) — single-row max 999.99h, not a 24h ceiling (single TimeEntry, not daily aggregate); dat032/033 adds hours<=24 CHECK
audit_logs immutability (dat009): BEFORE UPDATE OR DELETE trigger created AFTER all backfill UPDATEs — ordering is correct and documented
audit_logs immutability: trigger function uses ERRCODE='check_violation' (23514) distinguishable from role denial (42501)
audit_logs ON DELETE NO ACTION: prevents cascade DELETE from users hitting immutability trigger (SetNull path correctly removed)
dat007: tasks/project_snapshots/documents/time_entries FK switched from CASCADE/SetNull to RESTRICT — hard-delete of project with history is now blocked at DB level
dat007: Epic/Milestone/ProjectMember/ProjectClient/ProjectThirdPartyMember intentionally stay CASCADE (operational link data, not audit history) — verified consistent with migration rationale
obs012 deployments table: additive CREATE TABLE only, no FK (deployedBy is frozen string, survives user deletion), indexed on deployedAt and (environment, deployedAt)
dat021 schemaVersion ADD COLUMN NOT NULL DEFAULT 1: Postgres PG11+ stores the default in catalog without table rewrite — no row-level UPDATE, no immutability trigger fire
dat021 GIN index on audit_logs(payload jsonb_path_ops): correct operator class for containment @> queries; btree on schemaVersion for dispatch
dat021 hash-chain consequence: acknowledged that recompute script must run post-migration; pre-existing rows verify only after recompute (documented)
dat003/004: 14 CHECK constraints cover date-range ordering (>=) and numeric bounds; NULL semantics (CHECK passes when NULL) correctly noted for nullable columns
dat003: leaves.days > 0 strict positive (product invariant: calculateLeaveDays floored at 0.5); leave_balances.totalDays >= 0 allows zero balance
dat003: events.recurrenceEndDate >= date (nullable column — NULL check passes cleanly per SQL 3VL)
dat012: String→enum promotion uses DROP DEFAULT / ALTER TYPE USING / SET DEFAULT pattern correctly; no missing default transitions
dat012: AppSettingsCategory enum values ('display','general','planning') — lowercase matches existing data; default 'general' re-set correctly
dat013: HH:MM format CHECK regex '^([0-1]?[0-9]|2[0-3]):[0-5][0-9]$' is lenient superset of DTO regex — no app-accepts/DB-rejects mismatch; rejects '9:5', '25:99', '' as stated
dat013: NULL time columns pass CHECK (NULL semantics) — no spurious NOT NULL constraint introduced
dat014: leaves_sync_type_from_config BEFORE INSERT OR UPDATE trigger derives type from leaveTypeId FK — eliminates dual source of truth; one-time backfill runs before trigger attach
dat014: enum_range(NULL::"LeaveType") used instead of hardcoded list — future enum members auto-included
dat014: known limitation (config-side code change does not propagate to existing leaves) documented in migration; out of scope for this check
dat016: departments_name_key UNIQUE globally; services_departmentId_name_key composite UNIQUE per department — matches app semantics
dat017: tasks_parent_requires_project_ck CHECK (projectId IS NOT NULL OR (epicId IS NULL AND milestoneId IS NULL)) — NULL semantics correctly analyzed in comment
dat018: task_dependencies_no_self_ck CHECK taskId <> dependsOnTaskId; cycle-prevention trigger uses RECURSIVE CTE UNION (dedup terminates on pre-existing cycles)
dat018: UPDATE correctness — OLD row excluded via (TG_OP='INSERT' OR id <> OLD.id) in both CTE arms
dat023: EXCLUDE USING gist with btree_gist — daterange(...,'[]') inclusive bounds match checkOverlap inclusive semantics
dat023: partial WHERE (status='APPROVED') — re-checks on PENDING→APPROVED transition (the TOCTOU race path) and allows PENDING/REJECTED/CANCELLED overlaps
dat023: CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS is idempotent; superuser requirement documented in migration comment and deploy doc
dat032: subtasks_position_ck CHECK position >= 0 — no negative positions
dat033: time_entries_hours_ck CHECK hours >= 0 AND hours <= 24 — admits hours=0 for dismissal rows (101 in dev), correct superset of DTO bound
dat033: TOCTOU aggregate cap (concurrent same-day entries summing past 24h) acknowledged as out of scope — correct assessment; cross-row CHECKs not expressible per-row
dat036: clients_name_key UNIQUE — closes DAT-016 gap; redundant non-unique clients_name_idx dropped first
dat038: events_parent_no_self_ck uses IS DISTINCT FROM (tolerates NULL parentEventId); cycle trigger has NULL short-circuit on hot path
dat038: UPDATE correctness — OLD row excluded via (TG_OP='INSERT' OR e.id <> OLD.id) in both CTE arms; mirrors DAT-018 learning #3
dat035: project_members_role_length_ck CHECK char_length(role) BETWEEN 1 AND 100 — whitespace-only acknowledged as out of scope; DTO layer trims
dat037: tasks_project_consistency_trg skips comparison when projectId IS NULL (defers to DAT-017 CHECK) — no signal shadowing
dat037: epics/milestones cascade triggers fire AFTER UPDATE OF projectId only (not on INSERT) — no spurious cascade on initial project assignment
dat010: four composite/range indexes on leaves table (userId+status, validator_id+status, startDate+endDate, leave_type_id+status) — additive, no lock concern on small HR table
